paragraphs.body: Upon actually getting to grasp this bottle, the cap magnetically was snapped into place. The resistance was noticeable. Elegant and classy. It was like a door sealing shut a secret that hadn’t been revealed yet. I picked up the bottle, with the weight being unexpected and cool to the touch. I saw my fingerprint embossing itself into the bottle as I repositioned it in my hand. This was my first time being able to get my hands on this fragrance, and besides the actual lavender juice inside that I was excited to smell, the cap of the bottle was fascinating. The room was fancy, but this bottle was fancier. It is like the room was positioned for his bottle to be main stage gleaming in all of the light and glory. It was so unfamiliar yet inviting. It glistened and shined with a design I can’t describe as any way other than silver being poured out and hardened instantly as it landed. Abstract yet sleek. I broke the magnetic seal of the bottle and was hit with the most pleasant aroma that can only be described as a beautifully vibrant and juicy blend of grapefruit, bergamot, and ginger. It’s almost like I could see its lavender silage roaming in the air. When I was done, I placed the cap back on, now in a different position than it once was, and let the weight of the cap re-align itself with the bottle from the magnetic seal it has. The weight of the cap and the bottle now led my hand to land it back on its stand for others to admire.

annotations.text: It was like a door sealing shut a secret that hadn’t been revealed yet.

annotations.comment: Excellent details throughout this paragraph! They're so specific and sensory they focus your reader on this bottle. Nice move :)
annotations.context_left: weight of the cap and the bottle now led my hand to land it back on its stand for others to admire.

paragraphs.body: The fragrance is called Symphony. I find that interesting because a symphony is not just a song or a melody. It is not a single piece of music you listen to or can hum by yourself. It is not just a noise, even though it is quite vibrant and loud. It also isn’t in the background. I’ve never heard somebody say they heard a symphony as background noise. A symphony doesn’t even have to be music. It can be referenced as a bunch of things working in harmony to make something full or complete. Something like this doesn’t happen out of the blue. It’s constructed and put together over time. A symphony takes time to build and time to completely understand. A symphony is many things coming together in unison to become one. You put together an orchestra with all the different instruments playing together in perfect harmony and now you have a symphony. In other instances, though, it can be events or actions being put together like a puzzle that have nothing to do with music, but when put together, they are vibrant and loud, yet elegant, like a symphony is. In my writing, the symphony I have made up is one of events and objects that all intertwine to make up a whole that works fluently together. This whole is that every detail of what I’ve written about defines a piece of me and who I am as a person. I want to be smooth, good at everything I do, and be the elegance in the room that people aspire to be.

annotations.text: want to be smooth, good at everything I do, and be the elegance in the room that people aspire to be.
annotations.context_left: I am as a person. I
nd objects that all intertwine to make up a whole that works fluently together. This whole is that

paragraphs.body: For me, a big part of my symphony is to be smooth. What is being smooth? Let’s talk about that: Definition: Having a uniform surface that isn’t rough. It originates from a Middle English word form from old English smoõ meaning free or roughness, polished, or soft. This word can also describe something that happens without resistance or difficulty. A saying such as “that was smooth” or “smooth sailing”. This version of the word originates from the Proto-German word “smothaz”. Overtime, this saying came to include things such as actions and not just texture.

The word smooth has evolved in drastic ways over centuries. Initially, smooth was used to describe the physical texture of objects and surfaces. In later dates though, it began to take on a metaphorical implication In certain contexts. The social implications of “smooth” are also used to describe human interactions. The action of “smoothness” or “being smooth” was influenced by types of art like literature, painting, etc. As these types of things could be refined pieces of worth, smooth became a compliment for them. This is also why today you hear the terms like “smooth jazz”.

annotations.comment: I'm really interested in this etymological study you're doing in this essay!
annotations.context_right: The word smooth has evolved in drastic ways over centuries. Initially, smooth was u

paragraphs.body: The word has many positive connotations like just explained. Smooth can mean to be effortless, calm, sophisticated, polished, refined, or pleasing.

To be smooth to me is to not crack under pressure and to show no signs of stress. Not only to do that, but for that to be what is happening. What I mean by this is that you aren’t just hiding your stress, you just aren’t feeling it. A person I look up to a lot for this is my dad. It seems like he is always on top of what he does even though it is difficult. You’d never know. He can manage everything he does so well, and it seems like everything just works. That is smooth.

annotations.text: person I look up to a lot for this is my dad. It seems like he is always on top of what he does even though it is difficult. You’d never know. He can manage everything he does so well, and it seems like everything just works. That is smooth.
annotations.comment: Great transitional move! This essay is reaching and stretching in really interesting ways :)

paragraphs.body: When I was in about seventh grade, I used to get almost sick everyday going to school because of how much stress I’d feel. I started to learn new ways to handle it. That was the beginning of my time of how to handle stress. Now, as a student in college, stress comes at me in many ways and in many different forms. I’ve tried to learn how to handle all of them.

I was talking to a friend once who’s dad went to medical school which is what I want to do. He told me his dad saw a study saying that the stress from medical school is taking years off some peoples lives. This hit me extremely hard. I want to be able to manage that and not crack under pressure. Being able to function smoothly under great deals of stress is how the best people succeed. Any great athlete, doctor, or any type of worker must face challenges and the ones that persevere are the best at what they do.
All of these things are part of the symphony that I imagine myself as. Being smooth is the key to my symphony.

annotations.text: All of these things are part of the symphony that I imagine myself as. Being smooth is the key to my symphony.

paragraphs.body: I try to embody every single one of those words. I like to be “smooth”. It is funny enough though, still when I think of smooth, I think of objects that have an effortless touch. One thing that comes to mind is the cap of the Louis Vuitton Symphony bottle is big in comparison to most other fragrance bottle caps. It is a sculptural marvel made of some shiny silver material. It is as smooth as a river stone yet is weighted down with intention. It gleams and glistens under light, reflecting silvers like a reflection in a mirror. The surface is as sleek as ice, with unpredictable curves and edges that seamlessly flow into each other like a river with a laminar flow. The cap has a surprising weight to it when it is in your hand. It has the same weight as one of those small water bottles. Running your fingertip across its surface, there is no resistance, only an uninterrupted glide until your finger comes off of the cap. The cap finds its way onto the bottle seamlessly, as you guide the gap closer, the weight lets you drop the cap onto the bottle as it magnetically snaps together and is now connected to its home base. On impact, you feel the vibration of the heavy cap hitting the solid bottle and the magnetic seal closing, and every time, there is a dense click sound, letting you know that the objects aren’t hollow. When lifted, there is a moment of tension as you are breaking that magnetic seal, like opening a fridge door. The cap possesses a smell of the fragrance it is connected to, simple yet elegant, it smells like a beautiful blend of juicy grapefruit, fresh bergamot, and Inviting ginger. The cap looks as if you were to take molten silver from a bucket and start pouring it out from above, and it hardened in an instant, building on top of each other quickly.

annotations.text: it smells like a beautiful blend of juicy grapefruit, fresh bergamot, and Inviting ginger. The cap looks as if you were to take molten silver from a bucket and start pouring it out from above, and it hardened in an instant, building on top of each other quickly.

paragraphs.body: What I find most fascinating about “smooth” is that other things that you think are also smooth even in a different way define the word in all of the other definitions it has. The cap is smooth as in lack of texture, but it is also smooth as it takes the center of attention in a room looking elegant while doing so. It has a status and a power to it. All the positive connotations of smooth is what it has. Everybody wants what this cap has. I said in the beginning of this writing that I wanted to me all the positive connotations of smooth, but so is this little bottle cap. This single object embodies everything I want to be. It has taken all the attributes I want to be and has smoothly combined them all into one inanimate object, the Louis Vuitton Symphony cap. Work Cited:
